title: Codebase Audit Remediation — Dead Code & Security Gaps (R035)
scope:
Remediate every finding raised in research paper R035 — Codebase Audit: Dead Code & Security Gaps in priority order S5 → S2. This plan executes the audit's recommendations as 27 shippable stories spanning four feature areas: FE-SEC-0009 (security), FE-DEV-0020 (dead-code/dev cleanup), FE-AUD-0004 (audit-log gaps), and FE-SQL-0017 (migration reorg).

Sequencing follows the R035 Story Risk Review: Wave 1 LOW-risk (10 stories: gitignore, deletes, package prune, docs) → Wave 2 MED-risk (12 stories: endpoint contracts, audit writes, migrations, rate limits) → Wave 3 HIGH-risk (5 stories behind feature flags where possible: middleware, auth, session, WS gating).
value:
• Closes the 8 highest-severity (S5) gaps surfaced by the R035 deep-scan, including unauthenticated /ws upgrades and the spoofable clientIP override.
  • Removes confirmed-dead code paths so future grep results, bundle size, and audit findings stop carrying noise.
  • Adds the missing audit_log writes that compliance reviewers need to reconstruct privilege changes.
  • Establishes a sequencing pattern (LOW → MED → HIGH) that lowers the chance any single fix breaks production.
implementation_plan: Wave 1 (LOW risk, 10 stories): execute mechanical cleanup — .gitignore, dead-file deletes, package prune, doc moves, screenshots, docs/screenshots. Surface any false positives from build/CI before moving on.

Wave 2 (MED risk, 12 stories): land endpoint-level contract changes (rate limits, /healthz redact, FRONTEND_ORIGIN required, cookie unify), audit-log wiring, dead-tsx removal, migration reorg. Each behind its own PR with focused tests.

Wave 3 (HIGH risk, 5 stories): middleware/auth/session changes (00346 WS gate, 00348 clientIP whitelist, 00354 InsecureSkipVerify removal, 00367 session revoke, 00368 rate-limit composition). Land each behind a feature flag where supported; require integration-test sign-off before deploy.
After each wave: re-run npm run lint:* + go vet ./... + full test suite; update technical-debt register if any S2/S3 item is deferred.
Close the plan once all 27 cards reach Completed and a follow-up audit confirms the R035 findings no longer reproduce.
areas_impacted: SEC: auth middleware, /ws handshake, cookie attributes, CORS config, /healthz body, sanitiser
DEV: .gitignore, package.json, repo layout, screenshots, dead-file removals, branch hygiene
AUD: audit_log writes for feature-flag toggles and role/permission edits
SQL: migration filename reorg + schema_migrations backfill
feature_list: Composed per-user rate limit on top of per-IP
Cookie attribute parity across login + refresh
FRONTEND_ORIGIN required at boot (no wildcard fallback)
audit_log rows for feature-flag toggles + role/permission edits
Outbound WS proxy with verified TLS
Repo cleanup: 8 dead .tsx, 6 unused packages, scaffold/duplicate/fixture/webhook drafts, dead exports/types
features_removed: InsecureSkipVerify on outbound WS proxy dialer
Wildcard CORS fallback when FRONTEND_ORIGIN is unset
build_sha + env fields from public /healthz body

work_item_backlog.title: Require server-side auth on env-switch endpoints
Whitelist clientIP override to dev-tunnel CIDR only
Move Sec Audit/ out of repo root
Remove InsecureSkipVerify on outbound WS proxy
Unify cookie attributes across login and refresh paths
Redact build SHA and env from /healthz output
Emit audit events for role and permission edits
Revoke active sessions when role changes
Compose per-user rate limit on top of per-IP
Cleanup bundle: dead exports + unused types
Reorganise migrations into ordered numbered series
Prune stale GitHub branches and add backup retention policy

risks.risk: HIGH-risk middleware/auth changes (00346, 00348, 00354, 00367, 00368) regress on every authenticated request
risks.mitigation: Feature-flag the change where possible; require dedicated integration tests; land in Wave 3 only after Waves 1+2 stabilise CI signal.

risks.risk: Migration renumber (00370) collides with already-applied schema_migrations entries in long-lived environments
risks.mitigation: Backfill schema_migrations with the new numbers in the same migration; verify on a fresh DB and a staging clone before running on dev.

risks.risk: Dead-file deletions (00351, 00360–00362, 00364, 00369) miss a dynamic loader reference
risks.mitigation: Run npm run lint:* and full build after each deletion batch; revert any deletion the type-checker or runtime flags.

risks.risk: Branch prune (00372) removes a branch holding uncommitted work
risks.mitigation: Per HARD RULE, no destructive git op without explicit operator confirmation; only delete branches with merged status and >30 days idle.

risks.risk: Rate-limit thresholds (00355, 00356, 00368) lock out legitimate users
risks.mitigation: Start at conservative limits with metrics; tighten only after 1 week of production telemetry; expose a dashboard so ops can spot 429 spikes.
